articles.title: Severe heatwave grips Raj, Barmer hottest in country at 48.3°C - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Jaipur: Severe heatwave conditions continued to sweep across
Rajasthan Tuesday, with Barmer emerging as the hottest place in the country after recording a blistering maximum temperature of 48.3°C, according to the Meteorological Centre Jaipur. The intense heat across western Rajasthan pushed temperatures several notches above normal, making life difficult for residents in the desert region.
Not only did Barmer top the list of the country’s hottest places, Jaisalmer also figured among the hottest locations nationally after recording a maximum temperature of 45.8°C, reflecting the firm grip of severe heatwave conditions across western Rajasthan.
Barmer was followed by Phalodi, where the mercury touched 46.4°C. Chittorgarh recorded 45.6°C, Bikaner 45.0°C, Kota 44.7°C and Jodhpur at 44.6°C. Pilani and Churu registered 44.5°C each, while Fatehpur recorded 44.0°C. Sri Ganganagar reported 43.5°C, Udaipur 43.1°C, Dausa 43.0°C and Ajmer 42.7°C.
Jaipur too experienced intense heat during the day with the minimum temperature settling at 29.1°C in the morning while the daytime temperature climbed to 43.2°C. Major roads and marketplaces wore a deserted look during the afternoon as people largely remained indoors to avoid exposure to the scorching sun.
In the Walled City area, Jaipur Municipal Corporation(JMC) deployed smog guns to sprinkle water in an attempt to provide relief from the oppressive heat.
In contrast to the prevailing dry weather across most districts, some places in Alwar and Kotputli-Behror witnessed hailstorm activity and light rainfall during the afternoon, bringing temporary respite to residents.
The Met office warned that heatwave conditions are likely to intensify further over the next couple of days. Several eastern districts may continue to experience above-normal temperatures. Authorities advised people to avoid direct exposure to sunlight during peak afternoon hours and stay hydrated amid the continuing extreme weather conditions.

articles.title: Rajasthan issues season’s first red heat alert: Mercury crosses 48°C, IMD advises people to stay indoors - Bhaskar English

articles.full_text:
Rajasthan issues season’s first red alert for heat:Mercury crosses 48°C, IMD advises people to stay indoors
- Copy link
Rajasthan continues to face intense heatwave conditions. On Tuesday, Barmer remained the hottest city in the country for the third consecutive day, with the daytime temperature soaring to 48.3 degrees Celsius, the highest recorded in the state this season so far.
The Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for severe heatwave conditions in three districts on Wednesday (May 13). Authorities have also advised people to avoid stepping outdoors during the afternoon. Heatwave warnings remain in place for the next three days. Except for northern districts, weather conditions remained clear across most parts of Rajasthan during the last 24 hours.
Key updates related to the heatwave
Fear of temperatures touching 50°C- In the desert district of Barmer, the daytime temperature has crossed 48 degrees Celsius. Weather experts fear the mercury could touch 50 degrees within the next few days.
Tuesday’s temperature was the highest recorded this season. In Barmer, currently the hottest city in the country, temperatures have risen by nearly two degrees over the past two days.
Red alert issued for severe heat- The Meteorological Department issued its first red alert of the season for severe heatwave conditions on Wednesday. The alert has been issued for Barmer, Jaisalmer and Jodhpur.
Residents in these districts have been advised not to leave their homes during the afternoon hours. Severe heatwave warnings have also been issued for Thursday.
Temperatures above 40°C across Rajasthan- On Tuesday, every major city in Rajasthan recorded temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius. Amid rising temperatures, the Meteorological Department advised residents to avoid direct sunlight and remain hydrated by drinking water frequently.
Recorded temperatures included-
Weather conditions in major Rajasthan cities
Jaipur
Although a storm and rain alert had been issued for the capital on Monday, there was no significant change in weather conditions.
The sky remained clear throughout the day and hot winds continued to blow. Jaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 43.2 degrees Celsius on Tuesday, while the minimum temperature settled at 29.1 degrees Celsius.
Ajmer
Ajmer witnessed intense heat throughout the day on Tuesday. Humidity levels also increased due to light rainfall over the past few days.
Roads appeared deserted during the afternoon because of the heatwave conditions. The Meteorological Department recorded a maximum temperature of 42.7 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 29.8 degrees Celsius.
Kota
The spell of severe heat continued in Kota as well. Daytime temperatures rose by two degrees, while night temperatures increased by one degree.
Residents struggled with strong sunlight and hot winds. The maximum temperature on Tuesday was recorded at 44.7 degrees Celsius, while the minimum stood at 28.3 degrees Celsius. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions on Wednesday.
Jodhpur
The mercury remained close to 44 degrees Celsius in Jodhpur for the third consecutive day.
People faced intense heat and scorching sunlight from 10 am to 6 pm. The impact was visible in the city’s main markets, which remained largely deserted during daytime hours. Several villages in the region are also facing a water crisis.
Sikar
Tuesday was the hottest day of the season in Sikar. Strong sunlight and heatwave winds continued throughout the day, pushing temperatures up to 44 degrees Celsius. Hot winds also persisted during the night.
After dusty winds during the day, cloud cover developed in the evening. Light drizzle was reported in the Fatehpur area later in the day.
Alwar
Dense clouds covered Alwar city and surrounding areas during the afternoon, followed by light rainfall that brought some relief from the heat. The maximum temperature, which had reached 40 degrees Celsius on Monday, dropped to 39 degrees Celsius on Tuesday.
Udaipur
Heat conditions continued to intensify in Udaipur. On Tuesday, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 43.1 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 25.6 degrees Celsius. On Monday, the maximum temperature had been 42.3 degrees Celsius, while the minimum was 25.9 degrees Celsius. Residents continued to struggle with the rising heat and strong sunlight across the city.
